title: StaleBranchesReport
description: Output of `bin/check-stale-branches.sh`. Categorises local branches by hygiene state: branches whose tip is reachable from the base branch (safe to delete), and branches whose last commit is older than the configured threshold AND that aren't merged (potentially abandoned). Consumed by `bin/doctor.sh` (folds counts into the drift display + exit code) and by `bin/cleanup-branches.sh` (prunes the merged_into_base set when invoked with `--yes`).

target.description: Repo path the report was run against.

base_branch.description: Branch the merge check was run against. Defaults to the profile's primary base branch (`branching.base_branches[0]`).

current_branch.description: Caller's current branch — never reported as stale or merged regardless of state. Empty string when the repo is in detached-HEAD.

days_threshold.description: Age cutoff (in days) used for the `stale_unmerged` set. From profile.branching.stale_after_days, default 90.

merged_into_base.description: Branches whose tip commit is reachable from the base branch. Safe to delete locally — the work is already integrated.

last_commit_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the branch tip's commit.

stale_unmerged.description: Branches whose last commit is older than `days_threshold` AND that are NOT reachable from the base branch. Likely abandoned; the user should decide whether to revive (rebase + push) or delete.

skipped.description: True when the report could not run (e.g. base branch doesn't exist locally). When true, both arrays are empty and the doctor probe contributes no drift.

skip_reason.description: Human-readable reason when `skipped` is true. Absent when `skipped` is false.
